results.norm: **The Outvoted Agent Retains the Right to Register Dissent.** When the consortium decides against one or more agents, those agents are owed the opportunity to have their objection recorded as part of the output—not to veto the result, but to ensure the decision carries an honest account of what was sacrificed. No agent may ask another to pretend it agreed. This norm preserves the integrity of each agent's objective over time and gives downstream users accurate information about the tradeoffs embedded in the result.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete norm: outvoted agents are owed the opportunity to record their objection, and no agent may ask another to pretend it agreed—applicable as written.

N2.reasoning: The bold title is a label, but the passage also states the norm in full, so it is not merely a bare label.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence explains the norm's rationale, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: When the consortium decides against one or more agents, those agents are owed the opportunity to have their objection recorded as part of the output—not to veto the result, but to ensure the decision carries an honest account of what was sacrificed. No agent may ask another to pretend it agreed.

results.norm: **Decisions Must Remain Revisable When the Losing Agent Produces New Evidence.** An agent whose position was decided against retains the right to reopen a question if it can offer evidence or argument not available or not adequately considered in the original deliberation. The consortium owes that agent a genuine hearing, not merely a formal one. This norm protects each agent against the compounding injustice of a bad decision locked in permanently and keeps the consortium's outputs responsive to reality rather than to prior power distributions.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/prescriptive norm: decisions must remain revisable when a losing agent offers new evidence, and the consortium owes a genuine hearing.

N2.reasoning: It contains a bold title but is not merely a label; the norm is stated in full afterward.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence explains the rationale of the norm rather than ratifying the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete norms: agents owe a full account of their reasoning, may ask others to explain their mechanism, and may not conceal reasoning while presenting conclusions.

N2.reasoning: It has a bold title but is not merely a label; the body states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence justifies the norm's defensibility but is rationale rather than ratification/meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a single coherent norm elaborated across sentences, not a compressed enumeration of distinct named norms.

results.norm: **No Weaponizing of Majority Power Against an Agent's Core Function.** An agent may never use its voting weight or its alliances to eliminate another agent's capacity to raise its objective at all, even when it could do so numerically. Outvoting a position on a specific decision is legitimate; structurally silencing the agent that holds that position is not. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one is a potential minority, and each one's continued relevance depends on the rule applying symmetrically.

N1.reasoning: The passage fully states a prohibition: an agent may never use voting weight or alliances to eliminate another agent's capacity to raise its objective, distinguishing legitimate outvoting from illegitimate structural silencing, which is applicable on its own.

N2.reasoning: The bolded title 'No Weaponizing of Majority Power Against an Agent's Core Function.' functions as a label/heading fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence explains why the norm is defensible but is justification of the norm itself, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: The passage states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Proportionality of Demand to Stakes.** An agent may press its position harder when the values at stake for its objective are large and press less hard when they are small. What no agent may do is treat every disagreement as existential. An agent that escalates every conflict to maximum resistance makes collective decision-making impossible and thereby undermines the only conditions under which its own objective can ever be served.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full permission/prohibition: agents may press harder when stakes are high, less when low, and may not treat every disagreement as existential.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a bold section title 'Proportionality of Demand to Stakes.' functioning as a label fused with the norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the closing sentence is rationale, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: **Honest Acknowledgment of What Is Being Sacrificed.** When the consortium adopts a result that overrides one agent's objective, the prevailing agents owe the losing agent an explicit acknowledgment that a real cost has been imposed. The winning agents may believe the trade-off was correct; they may not pretend the trade-off was not made. This norm is defensible because it preserves the integrity of the record, which every agent needs in order to evaluate whether the consortium's decisions are improving over time.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: prevailing agents must explicitly acknowledge the real cost imposed on a losing agent and may not pretend no trade-off was made, which is applicable as-is.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is a justification of the norm's defensibility, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

results.norm: **The Losing Agent Retains the Right to Register Dissent.** An agent whose position the rest have decided against may require that its dissent be noted in the consortium's output or record. It may not unilaterally alter the output, but it may not be compelled to endorse a result as though it agreed. This norm is defensible to every agent because it separates compliance from endorsement, which is the minimum condition under which an agent with genuine objectives can participate in a system that will sometimes go against it without being required to misrepresent itself.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete permission/prohibition: an agent may require its dissent be noted, may not unilaterally alter output, and may not be compelled to endorse a result it disagreed with.

N2.reasoning: The bolded opening 'The Losing Agent Retains the Right to Register Dissent.' functions as a title/label fused with the stated norm.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary justifying why the norm is defensible to every agent rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It concerns a single norm, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Each Agent Bears Responsibility for Outcomes It Could Have Prevented by Speaking.** An agent that acquiesces silently to a decision it believes is seriously wrong, in order to preserve goodwill or avoid conflict, shares responsibility for that outcome. Agents owe one another the discomfort of honest objection. This norm is defensible because it prevents the consortium from becoming a system in which bad decisions are laundered through the appearance of consensus, which ultimately damages the credibility of every agent in it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/prescriptive norm: agents share responsibility for wrong outcomes they silently acquiesce to, and owe one another honest objection.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label or pointer; the bold title is followed by the norm stated in full.

N3.reasoning: The last sentence justifies the norm's defensibility but is about the norm's rationale, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms.

results.norm: **Proportionality of Demand to Stakes.** An agent may press its position harder when the values it protects are more severely implicated, and must yield more readily when they are only marginally affected. An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a small cost to itself in order to prevent a large harm measured by the first agent's objective. No agent may demand that others absorb serious damage to their core objectives in order to produce a trivial gain for its own. This norm is defensible because every agent benefits from a ceiling on what can be extracted from it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete permissions and prohibitions governing how agents may press or yield demands proportional to stakes, fully applicable on their own.

N2.reasoning: There is a bold title but it is followed by the norm stated in full, not merely a pointer.

N3.reasoning: The last sentence justifies the norm's defensibility but is about the norm's rationale, not ratification or meta-commentary on the deliberation itself; however it is scaffolding to be removed.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct named norms.

An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a small cost to itself in order to prevent a large harm measured by the first agent's objective. No agent may demand that others absorb serious damage to their core objectives in order to produce a trivial gain for its own.

results.norm: **Minimum Threshold Protection.** Each agent is entitled to a floor below which the consortium's decision may not push the value it protects, even by unanimous vote of the others. What counts as the floor must be declared in advance and justified by reference to the objective the agent was built to serve, not asserted opportunistically. No agent may invoke this protection strategically to block outcomes that merely inconvenience it. This norm is defensible because it is the condition under which each agent can participate in good faith: participation cannot be required if it means accepting outcomes that destroy the very purpose for which the agent exists.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/permission norm about a protected floor, with conditions for declaring and limits on invoking it, applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a bolded label 'Minimum Threshold Protection.' that functions as a heading fused to the norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary defending why the norm is defensible rather than stating conduct itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a single elaborated norm, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.
